questions.answer: Share capital refers to the total amount of money received by the company from its shareholders in exchange for the issue of shares. It forms the initial capital of the company.
key_points: Share capital is the initial capital of the company

questions.q: What is the difference between authorized capital and issued capital?
questions.answer: Authorized capital is the maximum amount of capital that a company is authorized to raise from its shareholders, whereas issued capital is the actual amount of capital raised by the company from its shareholders.
key_points: Authorized capital is the maximum amount of capital
Issued capital is the actual amount of capital

questions.q: What is the process of issuing shares to the public for the first time?
questions.answer: The process of issuing shares to the public for the first time is known as Initial Public Offering (IPO).

questions.q: What is the difference between equity shares and preference shares?
questions.answer: Equity shares give the shareholders the right to vote and participate in the profits of the company, whereas preference shares do not give voting rights and have a fixed dividend rate.
key_points: Preference shares do not give voting rights

questions.q: What is the journal entry for issuing shares at par?
questions.answer: The journal entry for issuing shares at par is Debit Cash A/c and Credit Share Capital A/c.

questions.q: What is the journal entry for issuing shares at a premium?
questions.answer: The journal entry for issuing shares at a premium is Debit Cash A/c and Credit Share Capital A/c and Debit Premium on Share Capital A/c.
key_points: Debit Premium on Share Capital A/c

questions.q: What is the journal entry for issuing shares at a discount?
questions.answer: The journal entry for issuing shares at a discount is Debit Share Capital A/c and Credit Cash A/c and Credit Discount on Issue of Shares A/c.
key_points: Credit Discount on Issue of Shares A/c

questions.q: State the accounting treatment for forfeited shares.
questions.answer: When shares are forfeited, the amount received on forfeiture is transferred to Share Capital A/c and the amount of premium received is transferred to Premium on Share Capital A/c.
key_points: Transfer to Premium on Share Capital A/c

questions.q: What is the accounting treatment for reissue of forfeited shares?
questions.answer: When forfeited shares are reissued, the amount received on reissue is credited to Share Capital A/c and the amount of premium received is credited to Premium on Share Capital A/c.
key_points: Credit to Premium on Share Capital A/c

questions.q: State the journal entry for redemption of preference shares.
questions.answer: The journal entry for redemption of preference shares is Debit Preference Share Capital A/c and Credit Redeemable Preference Share A/c or Bank A/c.
key_points: Credit Redeemable Preference Share A/c or Bank A/c

questions.q: What is the accounting treatment for bonus shares?
questions.answer: When bonus shares are issued, the bonus shares are credited to Share Capital A/c and the amount of bonus shares is debited to General Reserve A/c or Profit and Loss A/c.
key_points: Debit to General Reserve A/c or Profit and Loss A/c

questions.q: State the journal entry for issue of shares at a premium through public issue.
questions.answer: The journal entry for issue of shares at a premium through public issue is Debit Cash A/c and Credit Share Capital A/c and Debit Premium on Share Capital A/c.
key_points: Debit Premium on Share Capital A/c
questions.topic: Issue of Shares at a Premium

questions.q: What is the accounting treatment for issue of shares to employees as stock option?
questions.answer: When shares are issued to employees as stock option, the fair value of the option is charged to Profit and Loss A/c and the number of shares issued is credited to Share Capital A/c.
key_points: Charge to Profit and Loss A/c

questions.q: State the accounting treatment for buyback of shares.
questions.answer: When shares are bought back, the amount paid for buyback is debited to Share Capital A/c and the amount is credited to Bank A/c or Cash A/c.
key_points: Credit to Bank A/c or Cash A/c

questions.q: What is the accounting treatment for issue of shares to the public through rights issue?
questions.answer: When shares are issued to the public through rights issue, the amount received is credited to Share Capital A/c and the amount of premium received is credited to Premium on Share Capital A/c.
key_points: Credit to Premium on Share Capital A/c
